reviews.review_description: The App is great for relaxing and calming the mind. I highly recommend it.
This app made me super dizzy
Unlike other similar apps that I have tried this one doesn’t seem to support using it while lying down. Meaning the there is no way to reset the view orientation

This is one of those games where you have a stressful day and and you're like dude i need some sleep of how to really stressful annoying day so what i did was i got on here i found this game and i was like hey this works really well it's super calming and i fell asleep in like 10 seconds when i got off so really just download it right now if you haven't already because it's very relaxing no complaints amazing game period

reviews.review_title: An utterly beautiful and relaxing App.
reviews.review_description: This made me feel totally relaxed after a very difficult day. Somehow it made me realize there was no point creating stress inside my head. I highly recommend it for anyone after a hard day at work, as a way to switch off before going to bed. It put me totally at ease. I love the way you just float through these beautiful places.
This app works fantastic for anxiety. The deep breaths calm, my vagus nerve all the time. It really really works. I love it so much.

For people having a stiff body, having anxiety, breathing shallow or just anyone that needs some focus and abdominal training. This app is simple but effective. 

To the devs. Please allow some customization for the sounds/music. Or just a simple on/ off. I want to listen to my own music. Yours is good but it's much of the same.

I have tried several other "breathing" apps. This one is way different,  way better, way unique! You put the controller on your stomach, and the magic begins. I suggest watching the quick tutorial first. The music so good, great quality, relaxing. You move with your breathing. On the journey surrounding has slow movement of things that make the journey more relaxing and fun. There are different themes to choose from and from short, medium to longer journeys.  I'm not really quite sure what I expected when I downloaded the app, but I couldn't have imagined how it is. I simply love it. I was just checking it out, but when I finished, seriously felt better. I think me and people in general have a difficult time breathing from your stomach, and this app with the controller on your stomach, and the graphics help you have better breathing technique,  and making it easy to do so. I highly recommend

Really surprised i liked this as thought it would be too boring for my busy brain but I love it, and it feels really finely tuned so when you breath out reaaaaaally slowly you move more. It’s great thanks for making it !

I've been using VR since 2016 and I attempt to try every meditation app for Quest and PCVR. I meditate daily and although I'm tolerant of apps that are rough around the edges I'm still fairly critical.

Flowborne does an exceptional job helping you focus on your breath. It employs a novel breathing mechanic that forces you to breathe in a specific way in order to progress through a rendered landscape. The graphics are artful enough to promote a relaxing experience, but simple and sparse enough to ignore if you happen to bliss out. Neither the graphics or the sound design competes with the mechanic, thereby allowing you to focus on your breath to maximum benefit.

And it's free. Flowborne has an abundance of content for you to return to if you enjoy the experience. They're giving away more content than they should if they hope to one day monetize the app IMHO. It's great. Grab it and use it.

reviews.review_title: Helped the kid in the 1st attempt
reviews.review_description: So happy it's free and we could just try and it worked. Nice world they created for the neurofeedback.
🌈🌈🌈🌈   Good for you & me  💫💫💫💫

I like it very much. It's so good!

I wish it had some more instructions on the breathing technique, because it's not clear to me how far in and out to breathe. Do I empty the stomach entirely? Just let it out, or force all of it out? How far do I breathe in?

Also, I would like to be able to favorite the different "levels", and get see some stats. It would be great if it said when you did each level last, because then it's easier to mix it up from session to session.

Also, hand tracking would be great.

When it was on SideQuest, I seem to remember that there were some interesting quotes and ideas spread around in the levels. I would like an option to see them again.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We are currently evaluating which features and improvements we will integrate next, and your suggestions are very valuable. We already tried to capture the breath via hand tracking and the accuracy did not meet our high standards. However, this might improve with future updates, so we keep an eye on this matter. Stats and a more comprehensive UI are already planned.

reviews.review_description: The breathing mechanism works mostly as intended with only a few minor hiccups. I don't think it helped much with my meditation but the visuals were very pretty.
So far so good! I love that these platforms are using therapeutic modalities and will be using with my job

I really like Flowborne; it's my favorite app for meditation. But I miss two things:
1. The option to push the trigger to stop the advance, as existed in the demo version.
2. I think there are too many forms/entities popping up during journeys; I would like to have the possibility to select how many forms/entities I want to appear: "none", "a few" or "many" as now.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your positive feedback and the valuable suggestions how we could further improve the experience! We added your ideas to our roadmap and try to include them in the next update.

reviews.review_title: What Happened to the Original Storyline?
reviews.review_description:
Wasn't there a mystery of some woman and I needed to find messages along the way with hints?  I specifically remember that when I played the demo.  I don't mind that you guys decided to turn this into a meditation only app and take out the "Quest / Storyline" sort of other than in this day and age we have a thing called OPTIONS.

You KEEP the story mode and you also have a meditation only mode and let the people decide.  I feel like something I really liked about the original concept which made me come back and buy the game was taken from me. Kind of like how Trader Joe's used to sell this Blood Orange Juice - There was NOTHING like it.  AMAZING!  And then one day it was gone.  They stopped selling it.

Don't do that.  Release the friggen story back into the game. If you lost your story writer and that's why you stopped, visit my Twitch Stream at GeesePGZ and leave me a whisper and I'll be happy to help you out.  I am great at writing stories. I got a ZERO for Plagerism back when I was in like 10th grade because the Short Stories teacher was CONVINCED that I stole my story out of a book someone else wrote even though 100% I wrote it myself. I never went back to that class.  Luckily it was 6th hour so I just left school early that year.

How about that storyline?
Thank you! Great graphics, calming sounds, lovely places. Free. If I could I give you a 100 stars. A must have apk in your oculus.

I absolutely love this breathing application. I use it every night before bed and sometimes during the day. If there was someway to buy more places/environments, I most definitely would! I would love to buy a premium version if one was offered.

reviews.review_title: love this.  was so relaxing with calming
reviews.review_description: music and visuals helped me relax.  i almost fell asleep. in a good way!
